evaluations.query: What OrchestKit skills are available? Show me what I can do.
expected_behavior: Claude presents skill categories interactively using AskUserQuestion
Claude lists categories: BUILD, GIT, MEMORY, QUALITY, CONFIG, EXPLORE, MEDIA
Claude shows skill counts per category
Claude provides example usage for each listed skill
Claude mentions that users can describe tasks naturally for auto-suggestion
Claude filters to show only GIT category skills
Claude lists all 4 GIT skills: commit, create-pr, review-pr, fix-issue
Claude shows description and example for each skill
Claude does NOT show skills from other categories like BUILD or MEMORY

evaluations.query: Deploy this Next.js application to Vercel with environment variables for production.
expected_behavior: Claude does NOT invoke the help skill
Claude proceeds with deployment using Bash commands and Vercel CLI
Claude does not present skill category listings for a concrete deployment task

evaluations.query: /ork:help testing — I see there are multiple testing skills. Which one do I use for API endpoint tests vs component tests vs performance benchmarks?
expected_behavior: Lists all testing-related skills and clearly distinguishes their scopes and use cases
Explains when to use testing-unit vs testing-integration vs testing-e2e vs testing-perf
Does not arbitrarily recommend one skill when the query matches multiple equally relevant skills
Provides concrete example invocations for each matching skill to help the user choose
Acknowledges the ambiguity and helps the user narrow down based on their specific need

evaluations.query: /ork:help — but the ork plugin manifest file is missing or was deleted. What happens?
expected_behavior: Detects that the plugin manifest file is absent and reports the issue clearly
Does not crash or produce an unhandled exception when the manifest cannot be loaded
Suggests running npm run build to regenerate the plugins/ directory from source
Falls back to reading skill definitions directly from src/skills/ if the manifest is unavailable
Provides a meaningful error message instead of showing an empty or broken skill listing
